title: Uniface Installer for Windows
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on Microsoft Windows

Uniface Installer for Windows

Uniface Installer for Windows

The Uniface installer prompts you for the information it needs, and provides on-screen guidance to help you make the appropriate choices.

The installer is available in a 32-bit and 64-bit version and will install the Uniface IDE, Uniface Router, Uniface Server and related utilities.

When starting the installer, you will be asked to choose between a Typical or an Advanced installation. When choosing Typical, the installer will use default values, and will only ask for input for your license type.

When choosing Advanced, the installer will prompt you to select your configuration settings. The information you are asked to provide depends on the edition and the installer, but may include:

Requested Information Description
Installation Folder File system location where Uniface should be installed. By default, this is in C:/Program Files or C:/Program Files (x86).
Note: If you try to install over an existing installation, the installer will go into upgrade mode. Only versions 10.3.03 and 10.4.01 and higher can be upgraded, earlier versions require a new installation in a clean directory.
Features

Choose to configure IIS for use with Uniface.

Note: Only versions 10.4.02 and earlier have this feature.
Program Folder Name of the program folder in the Windows Start menu.
Location of User Data Location for the default SQLite database, compiled objects, and log files.
Java Runtime Environment (JRE) Configuration Choose whether to use the JRE provided by Uniface or an existing JRE installation.
Character Set Choose the character set or code page appropriate for your language.
Uniface Router Parameters Specify the names and port numbers to be used for the Uniface Router and Tomcat services. During installation, these server processes are installed as Windows services.

The installer checks whether the default port numbers are already in use and suggests the next available port numbers.
Tomcat Parameters
Uniface Server Account Name and password for the user account under which the Uniface Server runs. A default name and password are provided but you should change these to match your situation.
URouter Monitor Account Name and password for the privileged account to access the Uniface Router Monitor. The default values are provided but you should change these to match your situation.
License Type Choose your license type, and specify your Entitlement ID, license file, or license server. You can choose to configure manually later by editing the $LICENSE_OPTIONS settings.

It is possible to install Uniface products without user interaction. This can be useful when you are deploying Uniface applications to multiple users.
